model_narrative: We design a set of dice whose faces carry numeric values, arranged in a fixed cycle so that each die is paired against the next die in the cycle. For each die we choose the value on every face, and we decide, for each pairing of one of its faces against a face of the next die, whether that face is counted as a win. There is also a single common win count shared across the cycle. The objective is to maximize that common number of wins each die achieves against the next die.

sets.name: f
sets.doc: the faces on a single die, listed in increasing order; every die has this same set of face positions

sets.name: dice
sets.doc: the dice being designed, listed in a fixed cyclic order; the die after the last one wraps back to the first, so each die has a well-defined next die in the cycle

params.name: flo
params.doc: the lowest allowed value that may be assigned to any face of any die; a scalar equal to 1

params.name: fup
params.doc: the highest allowed value that may be assigned to any face of any die; a scalar equal to 18. The span of allowed values from lowest to highest, widened by one, serves as the large constant that switches the winning-relation inequalities on or off

vars.name: wnx
vars.doc: the common number of face-versus-face wins that every die scores against the next die in the cycle

vars.name: fval
vars.doc: the value printed on each face of each die; bounded between the lowest and highest allowed face values. The first face of the first die is fixed to the lowest allowed value to break symmetry

vars.name: comp
vars.doc: a win indicator over a die, one of its faces, and a face of the next die in the cycle; equals 1 when that face of the die is counted as beating that face of the next die, and 0 otherwise

description: For each die, the total number of winning face matchups it scores against the next die in the cycle must equal the single common win count shared by all the dice. Add up the win indicators over all pairings of that die's faces against the next die's faces and set that total equal to the common number of wins.

model_narrative: We design a set of dice whose faces carry numeric values, arranged in a fixed cycle so that each die is paired against the next die in the cycle. For each die we choose the value on every face, and we decide, for each pairing of one of its faces against a face of the next die, whether that face is counted as a win. There is also a single common win count shared across the cycle. The objective is to maximize that common number of wins each die achieves against the next die.

sets.name: f
sets.doc: the faces on a single die, listed in increasing order; every die has this same set of face positions

sets.name: dice
sets.doc: the dice being designed, listed in a fixed cyclic order; the die after the last one wraps back to the first, so each die has a well-defined next die in the cycle

params.name: flo
params.doc: the lowest allowed value that may be assigned to any face of any die; a scalar equal to 1

params.name: fup
params.doc: the highest allowed value that may be assigned to any face of any die; a scalar equal to 18. The span of allowed values from lowest to highest, widened by one, serves as the large constant that switches the winning-relation inequalities on or off

vars.name: wnx
vars.doc: the common number of face-versus-face wins that every die scores against the next die in the cycle

vars.name: fval
vars.doc: the value printed on each face of each die; bounded between the lowest and highest allowed face values. The first face of the first die is fixed to the lowest allowed value to break symmetry

vars.name: comp
vars.doc: a win indicator over a die, one of its faces, and a face of the next die in the cycle; equals 1 when that face of the die is counted as beating that face of the next die, and 0 otherwise

description: Whenever one of a die's faces is counted as beating a face of the next die in the cycle, that die's face value must be strictly greater than the other face's value, meaning at least one larger. This must hold for every combination of a die, one of its faces, and a face of the next die. When a matchup is not counted as a win the requirement must be allowed to fall away, so relax it using the available span of face values widened by one whenever the win indicator for that matchup is off.
expected_pyomo:
def eq3_rule(model, d, f, fp):
    dl = list(model.dice)
    nxt = dl[(dl.index(d) + 1) % len(dl)]
    return model.fval[d, f] + (model.fup - model.flo + 1) * (1 - model.comp[d, f, fp]) >= model.fval[nxt, fp] + 1
model.eq3 = Constraint(model.dice, model.f, model.f, rule=eq3_rule)

model_narrative: We design a set of dice whose faces carry numeric values, arranged in a fixed cycle so that each die is paired against the next die in the cycle. For each die we choose the value on every face, and we decide, for each pairing of one of its faces against a face of the next die, whether that face is counted as a win. There is also a single common win count shared across the cycle. The objective is to maximize that common number of wins each die achieves against the next die.

sets.name: f
sets.doc: the faces on a single die, listed in increasing order; every die has this same set of face positions

sets.name: dice
sets.doc: the dice being designed, listed in a fixed cyclic order; the die after the last one wraps back to the first, so each die has a well-defined next die in the cycle

params.name: flo
params.doc: the lowest allowed value that may be assigned to any face of any die; a scalar equal to 1

params.name: fup
params.doc: the highest allowed value that may be assigned to any face of any die; a scalar equal to 18. The span of allowed values from lowest to highest, widened by one, serves as the large constant that switches the winning-relation inequalities on or off

vars.name: wnx
vars.doc: the common number of face-versus-face wins that every die scores against the next die in the cycle

vars.name: fval
vars.doc: the value printed on each face of each die; bounded between the lowest and highest allowed face values. The first face of the first die is fixed to the lowest allowed value to break symmetry

vars.name: comp
vars.doc: a win indicator over a die, one of its faces, and a face of the next die in the cycle; equals 1 when that face of the die is counted as beating that face of the next die, and 0 otherwise

description: On any single die the face values must be strictly increasing from one face to the next in order, with each face at least one greater than the face before it. For every die and every face other than its first, the value on that face must be at least one more than the value on the immediately preceding face of the same die.
expected_pyomo:
def eq4_rule(model, d, f):
    fl = list(model.f)
    i = fl.index(f)
    if i == 0:
        return Constraint.Skip
    return model.fval[d, fl[i - 1]] + 1 <= model.fval[d, f]
model.eq4 = Constraint(model.dice, model.f, rule=eq4_rule)

model_narrative: We design a set of dice whose faces carry numeric values, arranged in a fixed cycle so that each die is paired against the next die in the cycle. For each die we choose the value on every face, and we decide, for each pairing of one of its faces against a face of the next die, whether that face is counted as a win. There is also a single common win count shared across the cycle. The objective is to maximize that common number of wins each die achieves against the next die.

sets.name: f
sets.doc: the faces on a single die, listed in increasing order; every die has this same set of face positions

sets.name: dice
sets.doc: the dice being designed, listed in a fixed cyclic order; the die after the last one wraps back to the first, so each die has a well-defined next die in the cycle

params.name: flo
params.doc: the lowest allowed value that may be assigned to any face of any die; a scalar equal to 1

params.name: fup
params.doc: the highest allowed value that may be assigned to any face of any die; a scalar equal to 18. The span of allowed values from lowest to highest, widened by one, serves as the large constant that switches the winning-relation inequalities on or off

vars.name: wnx
vars.doc: the common number of face-versus-face wins that every die scores against the next die in the cycle

vars.name: fval
vars.doc: the value printed on each face of each die; bounded between the lowest and highest allowed face values. The first face of the first die is fixed to the lowest allowed value to break symmetry

vars.name: comp
vars.doc: a win indicator over a die, one of its faces, and a face of the next die in the cycle; equals 1 when that face of the die is counted as beating that face of the next die, and 0 otherwise

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, for each die make the total number of winning face matchups it scores against the next die in the cycle equal the single common win count shared by all dice. Second, whenever one of a die's faces is counted as beating a face of the next die that face's value must be strictly greater, holding for every combination of a die, one of its faces, and a face of the next die, and relaxed using the span of face values widened by one whenever that matchup is not counted as a win. Finally, on each die make the face values strictly increasing from one face to the next, with every face after the first at least one greater than the preceding face on the same die.
expected_pyomo:
def eq1_rule(model, d):
    return sum(model.comp[d, f, fp] for f in model.f for fp in model.f) == model.wnx
model.eq1 = Constraint(model.dice, rule=eq1_rule)
def eq3_rule(model, d, f, fp):
    dl = list(model.dice)
    nxt = dl[(dl.index(d) + 1) % len(dl)]
    return model.fval[d, f] + (model.fup - model.flo + 1) * (1 - model.comp[d, f, fp]) >= model.fval[nxt, fp] + 1
model.eq3 = Constraint(model.dice, model.f, model.f, rule=eq3_rule)
def eq4_rule(model, d, f):
    fl = list(model.f)
    i = fl.index(f)
    if i == 0:
        return Constraint.Skip
    return model.fval[d, fl[i - 1]] + 1 <= model.fval[d, f]
model.eq4 = Constraint(model.dice, model.f, rule=eq4_rule)
